_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL House Broward/Miami-Dade HD-103..107 (Bartleman, F.Robinson, Woodson, Basabe, Aristide). State tier. All marquee roll calls verified against official FL Legislature House vote PDFs (SB 300 2023 6wk-ban, HB 543 2023 permitless carry, SB 1718 2023 immigration, HB 759 2025 gun-age). KEY CATCH: Fabian Basabe (R, HD-106) was 97/A by party-inference -- corrected to a documented MIXED maverick record: SKIPPED SB 300 + filed a 12-week loosening bill (life FALSE), self-IDs as LGBTQ community / pro-gay-rights (marriage q0/q1 FALSE), but voted YES on HB 1521 biological-sex/bathroom + HB 1069 Don't-Say-Gay-expansion (marriage q2/q4 TRUE), YES HB 1 vouchers, YES HB 543 carry, YES SB 1718 immigration. Also corrected a research error: F.Robinson SB 1718 was NAY (not YEA as one aggregator showed) -- verified against the official House PDF, so NO immigration deviation for her.

profile.education: BS emotionally-handicapped education, Florida International University (1994); MS emotional-disturbance education, Nova Southeastern University (1997)
profile.background: Former special-education teacher and assistant principal; Weston City Commissioner (2000-04); Broward County School Board (2004-20). In FL House since Nov 2020 (HD-104, redrawn to HD-103).

robin-bartleman@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-103 (Broward), Democrat, seeking reelection 2026 (not term-limited). Uniformly progressive; no conservative cross-party deviation found. All FALSEs verified against official FL House roll-call PDFs: NAY SB 300 (2023 6-week abortion ban), NAY HB 543 (2023 permitless carry), NAY SB 1718 (2023 immigration/E-Verify/anti-sanctuary), NAY HB 759 (2025 gun-age rollback -- filed amendments to keep the post-Parkland 21 age), NAY HB 1 (2023 universal vouchers, led opposition: 'coupon for millionaires'), NAY H 289 (2026 fetal personhood). Endorsed/backed by Planned Parenthood FL PAC and EMILY's List. No abortion-IVF, election-integrity, federalism, or public-justice record located -> null.

0.note: Voted NAY on SB 300 (2023) 6-week abortion ban and opposed the 2026 fetal-personhood bill; does not affirm personhood from conception in law.

1.note: Voted against even a 6-week restriction -- does not advocate abortion abolition.

4.note: Backed by Planned Parenthood FL PAC and EMILY's List (per Choice Tracker).
Voted NAY on HB 1557 (2022) Parental Rights in Education ('Don't Say Gay') -- does not oppose LGBTQ content in schools.

0.note: Voted NAY on HB 1 (2023) universal school-choice vouchers and led the Democratic opposition, calling it 'a coupon for millionaires.'

2.note: Voted NAY on SB 1718 (2023), which restricts sanctuary policies and strengthens immigration enforcement.

3.note: Voted NAY on SB 1718, which mandated E-Verify for larger employers.

0.note: Voted NAY on HB 543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry.

1.note: Voted NAY on HB 759 (2025) to keep the post-Parkland firearm-purchase age at 21 and filed amendments against the rollback -- supports gun restrictions.

profile.education: BS Mathematics, Tuskegee University (1994); MS Math Education, University of Miami (2005); EdS Educational Leadership, Barry University (2013)
profile.background: 25+ years Miami-Dade County Public Schools educator (math teacher, instructional coach, vice principal). Miami Gardens City Council 2010-18, Vice Mayor 2014-18. House Democratic Deputy Whip.

felicia-robinson@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-104 (Miami Gardens), Democrat, opened a 2026 reelection account (not term-limited). Consistent progressive; NO conservative deviation. All FALSEs verified against official FL House roll-call PDFs: NAY SB 300 (2023 6-week ban), NAY HB 543 (2023 permitless carry), NAY SB 1718 (2023 immigration enforcement), NAY HB 1 (2023 universal vouchers). NOTE: an aggregator (FastDemocracy) showed her SB 1718 vote as YEA -- the official House vote PDF confirms NAY, so there is NO immigration cross-party deviation; the contested claim was a research error, corrected here. Backed by Planned Parenthood FL PAC and EMILY's List. No marriage-specific roll call, election, federalism, or public-justice record located -> null.

0.note: Voted NAY on SB 300 (2023) 6-week abortion ban and opposed the fetal-personhood bill; pro-choice.

1.note: Voted against even a 6-week restriction -- does not advocate abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood FL PAC and EMILY's List (per Choice Tracker).

0.note: Voted NAY on HB 1 (2023) universal school-choice vouchers; called the program a vehicle for 'fraud, waste and abuse of public money.'

2.note: Voted NAY on SB 1718 (2023) anti-sanctuary/enforcement bill (official House PDF: 'N Robinson, F.-104'). Corrects an aggregator's erroneous YEA.

3.note: Voted NAY on SB 1718, which mandated E-Verify for larger employers.

0.note: Voted NAY on HB 543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry.

profile.education: AS public administration, Miami Dade College; BS criminal justice, Florida International University; MS public administration/management, St. Thomas University
profile.background: Born in Port-de-Paix, Haiti; immigrated to Florida 1981. Former Miami-Dade County public administrator. In FL House since 2020 (HD-101, redistricted to HD-105).

marie-paule-woodson@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-105 (Broward -- Hollywood/Pembroke Pines/Miramar), Democrat, running for reelection 2026 (not term-limited). Freedom Index lifetime ~13% (consistently progressive). All FALSEs verified against official FL House roll-call PDFs: NAY SB 300 (2023 6-week ban), NAY HB 543 (2023 permitless carry), NAY SB 1718 (2023 immigration enforcement), NAY HB 759 (2025 gun-age rollback). Filed a floor amendment against HB 1557 (2022 'Don't Say Gay'); publicly opposed HB 1 (2023) vouchers ('puts public education's role as a great equalizer at risk'). No Planned Parenthood/EMILY's endorsement confirmed -> abortion-funding cell null, not inferred. No election, federalism, or public-justice record located -> null.

0.note: Voted NAY on SB 300 (2023) 6-week abortion ban (official House PDF: 'N Woodson-105'); does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted against even a 6-week restriction -- does not advocate abortion abolition.

4.note: Filed a floor amendment against HB 1557 (2022) 'Don't Say Gay' and was in the opposition bloc -- does not oppose LGBTQ content in schools.

0.note: Publicly opposed HB 1 (2023) universal vouchers, warning it 'puts public education's role as a great equalizer at risk.'

2.note: Voted NAY on SB 1718 (2023) anti-sanctuary/enforcement bill (official House PDF: 'N Woodson-105').

3.note: Voted NAY on SB 1718, which mandated E-Verify for larger employers.

0.note: Voted NAY on HB 543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry (official House PDF: 'N Woodson-105').

1.note: Voted NAY on HB 759 (2025) -- supports keeping the post-Parkland gun-purchase age restriction.

profile.education: Cheshire Academy; studied international relations at Pepperdine University
profile.background: Former reality-TV personality and Miami Beach socialite; family restored the Boulevard/Breakwater hotels on Ocean Drive. In FL House since 2022. Self-describes as 'fiscally conservative and socially liberal' and a member of the LGBTQ community.

fabian-basabe@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-106 (Miami Beach/Bal Harbour), REPUBLICAN, seeking a third term 2026. CORRECTION: prior 97/A score was party-inferred and is contradicted by the record -- Basabe is a documented MIXED/maverick. LIFE DEVIATION (verified): did NOT vote on SB 300 (2023 6-week ban -- Not Voting in the official House PDF), publicly said both 6 and 15 weeks were wrong, and filed/withdrew HB 93 (2023) to LOOSEN the ban toward 12 weeks -- so he does not affirm personhood/abolition (FALSE). MARRIAGE: self-IDs as LGBTQ community / ran supporting gay rights (q0/q1 FALSE), BUT voted YES on HB 1521 (2023 biological-sex/'bathroom' bill) and YES on HB 1069 (2023 'Don't Say Gay' expansion) -> q2/q4 TRUE. GOP-aligned HARD votes: YES HB 1 (2023 universal vouchers, co-introducer), YES HB 543 (2023 permitless carry, official PDF 'Y Basabe-106'), YES SB 1718 (2023 immigration enforcement, official PDF 'Y Basabe-106'). No economic, election-integrity, federalism, or public-justice roll call located -> null.

0.note: Did not vote on SB 300 (2023) 6-week ban (Not Voting in official House PDF) and filed HB 93 to loosen the ban toward 12 weeks, saying both 6 and 15 weeks were wrong -- does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Proposed loosening the abortion ban to 12 weeks -- the opposite of advocating abortion abolition.

0.note: Self-identifies as a member of the LGBTQ community and 'socially liberal'; campaigned supporting gay rights -- does not affirm marriage as exclusively one man/one woman.

1.note: Self-identifies as LGBTQ and pro-gay-rights -- does not oppose same-sex marriage/civil unions.

2.note: Voted YES on HB 1521 (2023), the 'Facility Requirements Based on Sex' bathroom bill affirming biological sex in public facilities.

4.note: Voted YES on HB 1069 (2023), the expansion of the Parental Rights in Education ('Don't Say Gay') law restricting LGBTQ/gender-ideology instruction in schools.

0.note: Co-introducer of and voted for HB 1 (2023) universal school-choice voucher program.

2.note: Voted YES on HB 1069 (2023), which restricts gender-ideology/SOGI instruction and adds pronoun limits in K-12.
Voted YES on SB 1718 (2023) anti-sanctuary/enforcement bill (official House PDF: 'Y Basabe-106').

3.note: Voted YES on SB 1718, which mandated E-Verify for larger employers.

0.note: Voted YES on HB 543 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry (official House PDF: 'Y Basabe-106').

profile.education: BS Criminal Justice, Bethune-Cookman University (1988); MS Exceptional Student Education, Nova Southeastern University (2000)
profile.background: 35 years in Miami-Dade public education; 10+ years principal of Miami Northwestern Senior High (Principal of the Year 2014). Freshman in the FL House, elected Nov 2024.

wallace-aristide@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: FL HD-107 (Miami-Dade -- Miami Gardens/North Miami), Democrat, freshman (elected Nov 2024), running for reelection 2026. Thin but uniformly progressive record; no conservative deviation. HARD: voted NAY on H 289 (2026 fetal-personhood bill, per Choice Tracker) and NAY on HB 759 (2025 gun-age rollback, official House floor vote -- supports keeping the post-Parkland 21 age). Voted NAY on CS/HB 1205 (2025 ballot-initiative tightening), but that bill does not map to the election-integrity rubric cells (paper ballots/voter ID/mail-in/private funding) -> null, not forced. No marriage, education, federalism, or immigration position located beyond committee membership -> null (no fabrication).

0.note: Voted NAY on H 289 (2026) fetal-personhood bill -- does not affirm personhood from conception in law.

1.note: Voted NAY on HB 759 (2025) -- supports keeping the post-Parkland firearm-purchase-age restriction rather than easing gun limits.
